app.title: CE Editor
toolbar.reload_project_tip: Re-read the project from disk (last save); discards unsaved edits
toolbar.snapshot_tip: Revert the project to its snapshot (initial state); all edits are lost, including saved ones
toolbar.bi_export_tip: Export a CE Tool project: areaflags.map + cfglimits + per-flag TGA layers + XML project
dlg.no_missions_text: No missions in the folder (mpmissions/* with cfglimitsdefinition.xml or areaflags.map).
mission.title: {name}  ({world}, {size} m)
af.repaired: areaflags: CRLF corruption removed (−{n} bytes), data exact
src.mapfile_hint:
Unpack a map straight from the game files — no mission needed, just to look at it.
• DayZ game folder — unpacks the vanilla maps (Chernarus, Livonia).
• Mod folder (@Mod) — that mod's map; mods name the archive freely.
Point at the tile PBO yourself or press “Find” — the editor scans the archives
and keeps the ones that actually carry satellite tiles.
Editing tools stay disabled: a background has no areaflags.map.
af.widen_ask:
A cell of this map holds {bits} usage bits, so the flags {names} can be neither painted nor saved.

Widen the cell to 32 bits? The file is rewritten in full: bit numbers and every painted zone are preserved, and room appears for the remaining flags. The layout becomes the one vanilla maps use (chernarusplus, enoch, sakhal).

The usage layer in the file doubles (~+32 MiB). A backup is made before writing.
af.widen_done:
The cell is now 32 bits, the file was rewritten ({size} bytes) and verified against memory.
Backup: {backup}
af.widen_failed:
Could not widen the cell: {err}
The file is unchanged.
af.usage_blocked:
A cell of this map holds {bits} usage bits, and more flags are declared. These cannot be painted (the brush does not list them): {names}.
To make them work the map has to be rebuilt in CE Tool — the cell width is fixed when areaflags.map is generated.
af.usage_empty: The usage layer is empty: not a single cell carries a zone. This looks like a map template — zones are drawn by the server owner, while tiers may already be painted.
objects.hint: Click the map near a building
loot.hint: Select a building (Object Inspector must be on)
brush.mode_tip: LMB paints on the active layer; navigation stays on the right button
brush.tool_rect: Rectangle: drag, adjust by handles, Space to fill
brush.tool_ellipse: Ellipse: drag, adjust by handles, Space to fill
brush.tool_polygon: Polygon: click vertices, click the first point to close, Space to fill
brush.tool_lasso: Lasso: drag with LMB held, the contour closes itself, Space to fill
brush.apply_tip: Apply the contour to the active layer; a line (not a shape) is not applied
brush.layer_tip: Bitplane the brush paints on (enabled automatically)
brush.erase_tip: The brush clears the flag instead of setting it (Tab to toggle)
brush.replace_tip: Sets the active layer's flag and CLEARS every other enabled layer under the stroke (disabled layers are untouched)
brush.saved: No edits — data matches the file
brush.save_tip: Writes with a backup and verification; the server reads this file
save.title: Saving areaflags.map
save.crlf_note: The file will be saved as CLEAN v1: the CRLF corruption is removed (−{n} bytes). The data itself does not change.
save.changed_on_disk:
The file on disk changed after the map was loaded — the server may have rewritten it.

Saving will overwrite those changes. Write anyway?
exit.title: Exit
exit.unsaved:
There are unsaved edits: {n} cells.

Exit without saving?
export.title: Export
export.no_layers: No tier or usage layer is enabled — nothing to save.
export.no_zones: Pick a layer in the Layers panel first — its zones go to the CSV.
diff.hint: Compare the current map with another areaflags.map
diff.legend:
{add} added (in your map, not in the loaded one)
{rem} removed (in the loaded map, not in yours)
diff.header:
{src} (vs current)
{cells} cells changed ({pct} %), {ha} ha
Click a flag to see it on the map
diff.no_limits: No cfglimitsdefinition.xml next to the file — the flag bit order is unknown
toolbar.select_region_tip: Drag a box on the map — statistics and spawn for the region; a click without dragging clears it
items.hint: Check items to show their buildings on the map
items.limit: limit reached: no more than {max} items at once
items.only_spawnable_tip: Hide types that CE never spawns (nominal 0 or no category)
layers.toggle_tip: Show/hide the layer on the map
layers.bld_border_opacity_tip: Footprint outline opacity (crisp borders without fill)
ce.hint: Import a CE Tool project: fresh water and layer comparison with the live areaflags (read-only).
ce.loaded: {name}: {n} layers. Click a layer to overlay for comparison.
ce.water_tip: Fresh-water overlay (ponds/lakes) from water-fresh.tga; the point inspector gets a fresh-water mark
ce.mismatch: Project grid {proj} ≠ map grid {live} — different world?
inspector.origin_legend: g = from group, c = from map cell
zones.hint: Click a layer name in the Layers panel
zones.labels_tip: Number and area at the zone centre; small zones get labels as you zoom in
color_dlg.title: Layer color: {name}
lang.restart_hint: The interface language will apply the next time the editor starts.
welcome.subtitle: Loading a project
src.folder_root_label: DayZ server root folder or a mission folder:
src.bi_folder_label: CE Tool project folder (with the XML project and a layers folder):
src.bi_summary: Grid {grid}×{grid}, world {world} m · usage: {u} · value: {v} · layers: {n}
bgp.title: Background (satellite)
bgp.image_hint: Scaled to the world size from areaflags (km come from the map).
bgp.none_warn:
No background is selected. Adding one later will require editing the project config manually (config.json).

Create the project without a background?
src.mapfile_folder_dlg: Folder with game or mod files
src.mapfile_none: No PBO with satellite tiles in this folder.
src.mapfile_done: World “{world}” unpacked: background {size} m, zoom levels {zoom}; buildings — {classes} classes with footprints.
